headline: Security situation has deteriorated significantly, NDB warns.
summary: Switzerland's Federal Intelligence Service reports elevated threats across espionage (Russia and China maintain strong Bern-Geneva intelligence presence), terrorism (online jihadist radicalisation), cyberattacks on critical infrastructure, and proliferation/sanctions evasion by Russia, Iran, and Nor

headline: Human trafficking victims rose by ~20 in 2025.
summary: The FIZ victim-support NGO identified 82 trafficking victims in Switzerland during 2025, up roughly 20 from the previous year; 48 of 82 were in forced sex work.

headline: UNICEF: 45-point education gap by social background.
summary: A new UNICEF analysis finds 91% of Swiss 15-year-olds from privileged households achieve strong reading and mathematics scores; only 46% of peers from disadvantaged backgrounds do — one of the starkest within-country education equity gaps reported.

headline: Bilaterals III under parliamentary deliberation.
summary: Both chambers of the Federal Assembly are working through the Federal Council's March 13 dispatch on the Switzerland-EU Bilaterals III package — covering updated free movement, electricity, health, food safety, and Horizon/Erasmus participation.

headline: Nationwide domestic-violence hotline 142 live.
summary: Switzerland's new unified emergency number for domestic violence victims launched May 1 across all cantons, replacing fragmented cantonal lines.

headline: Trump flies to Beijing for Xi summit (May 14–15); Iran blockade tops agenda.
summary: Trump arrives in Beijing Wednesday evening for his first face-to-face with Xi since the October trade truce; bilateral sessions run Thursday–Friday.

headline: Ukraine: US-brokered 3-day ceasefire collapses amid mutual blame.
summary: The ceasefire announced May 8 (including a 1,000-prisoner swap per side) has lapsed; both Moscow and Kyiv accuse each other of violations.

headline: Strait of Hormuz blockade persists after Operation Epic Fury ends.
summary: US-Israel airstrikes on Iran (Operation Epic Fury) concluded May 5 following the April 8 ceasefire; Iran's hold on the Strait of Hormuz remains intact, sustaining global fuel inflation.

headline: India–Pakistan: one year of peace, zero diplomacy.
summary: A year after the May 2025 armed conflict and US-brokered ceasefire, there have been no incidents along the Line of Control.

headline: Netanyahu accused of using Iran war to reshape the region; April deadliest month in Gaza.
summary: Former Qatar PM and regional analysts argue Israel is leveraging the 2026 Iran war to restructure the Middle East order.

headline: JWST produces the most detailed cosmic-web map ever.
summary: A UC Riverside-led team used the COSMOS-Web JWST survey to map galaxy filaments across 164,000 galaxies, tracing large-scale structure back to z ≈ 7 (~1 billion years post-Big Bang).

headline: Cryptography advance: undecidable math as a secrecy tool.
summary: Quanta Magazine reports that a graduate student built a powerful new cryptographic primitive by harnessing the unknowability of undecidable mathematical proofs — a hardness source distinct from classical number-theoretic assumptions, potentially relevant to post-quantum cryptography design.

headline: Cambrian fossil trove rewrites early animal origins.
summary: Remarkably preserved fossils from southern China (~500 Mya, end-Cambrian) yield dozens of new species and revise models of the Cambrian explosion's timeline; half the species recovered are new to science.

headline: Non-standard genetic code evolving repeatedly in ciliates.
summary: Biologists studying microscopic pond organisms found multiple independent evolutionary reassignments of stop codons in ciliates, showing that the universal genetic code is more evolutionarily plastic than assumed — with implications for synthetic biology.

headline: Brown-fat molecular switch identified (McGill).
summary: McGill University researchers pinpointed a molecular "switch" activating the thermogenic calorie-burning network in brown adipose tissue.

headline: arXiv:2605.07046
summary: (May 2026) — *An Interpretable and Scalable Framework for Evaluating Large Language Models.* Reformulates LLM evaluation as constrained matrix factorisation, yielding orders-of-magnitude speedups over prior methods while matching accuracy.

headline: arXiv:2605.07886
summary: (submitted May 8, 2026) — *Characterizing and Correcting Effective Target Shift in Online Learning.* Proves that online kernel regression is equivalent to offline regression with implicitly shifted and inaccurate target labels; proposes a correction scheme.

headline: arXiv:2605.10471
summary: (submitted May 11, 2026) — *Quantum and classical processing with photonic quantum machine learning.* Reports a photonic quantum reservoir device capable of performing both quantum and classical ML tasks on the same hardware.

headline: arXiv:2605.10105
summary: (submitted May 11, 2026) — *Application of Machine Learning to 21 cm Cosmology.* Comprehensive review of ML-based parameter inference from redshifted 21 cm signals (Epoch of Reionisation), timed ahead of SKA telescope first light.

headline: Asian session mixed; Trump-Xi optimism vs. Iran oil pressure.
summary: Nikkei 225 +0.23% (62,888), KOSPI +0.99% (7,718), Hang Seng -0.38% (26,247), Shanghai Composite flat (4,214).

headline: EUR/CHF ~0.9167; USD/CHF ~0.780.
summary: EUR/CHF near 0.9167 (May 12 close); the pair ranged 0.9146–0.9176 over the past week.

headline: US futures lower pre-open; Iran re-escalation risk.
summary: S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 futures down ~1% overnight after Trump described an Iranian peace overture as "totally unacceptable," reviving fears of resumed military operations; Dow near flat.
